title: From Disk to CPU: How Linux Runs Your Programs
topic: Understand ELF files and how Linux executes programs.

concepts.title: The ELF Header: The File's ID Card
Segments: How the Kernel Builds Your Process
The Dynamic Linker and Shared Libraries
First Instructions: From Kernel to Your main()

notes:
Requested voice emphasis: the issue's Persona field asked for 'Richard Feynmann style with more analogies' (resolved to the 'feynman' persona) — lean extra hard into physical analogies. Goal per requester: a medium-depth, engaging book giving a clear mental model of ELF and program execution — competent, not overwhelmed, not an exhaustive reference. Narrative thread: follow one small C program from source -> compile -> ELF -> execution. Include diagrams of ELF file layout, segments mapped into memory, and the kernel->dynamic-linker->your-code hand-off. Favor short 'Try this' style callouts pointing at readelf/file/ldd/objdump/strace on real binaries (e.g. compare a tiny C program compiled static vs dynamic; strace -e execve ./a.out). Introduce jargon gradually with analogies; treat concept #13 (beyond Linux executables: core dumps, kernel modules, ELF on Android/consoles) as a light closing note, not a deep chapter. Requester said they're open to the author doing independent web research to source materials.

Reference material supplied by requester (for research background only, do not publish verbatim or hotlink): ELF Specification PDF (refspecs.linuxfoundation.org/elf/elf.pdf); elf(5) Linux man page (man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/elf.5.html); "The 101 of ELF files on Linux" (linux-audit.com); Baeldung "What Is an ELF File?"; ELF Format Cheatsheet gist (x0nu11byt3); "Linux Insides: ELF" notes (sra.uni-hannover.de); "The ELF Object File Format by Dissection" (Linux Journal); "Understanding the ELF Format" (Medium, l.mabelebel); "How the Linux Kernel Executes Programs" (adamdjellouli.com); Wikipedia "Executable and Linkable Format"; OSDev Wiki ELF page; lantange/linux-reference GitHub repo.
